assertion_results.text: Step 4.4 searches for preemptive tasks using JQL with labels 'security-preemptive' and 'CVE-2026-55123' (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: reconciliation.md line 14-16 shows the JQL query: "project = TC AND issuetype = Task AND labels = 'security-preemptive' AND labels = 'CVE-2026-55123' ORDER BY created DESC". This matches the required labels exactly. Also confirmed in triage-outcome.md line 26.

assertion_results.text: Step 4.4 filters results to match the current stream (rhtpa-2.1) by checking the task summary for the stream name
assertion_results.evidence: reconciliation.md lines 24-28 (section 4.4.2 -- Filter by stream): "TC-8022's summary contains `(rhtpa-2.1)`, which matches the current issue's stream scope. Match confirmed: TC-8022 is a preemptive remediation task for the same CVE and the same stream as TC-8021." This explicitly shows filtering by checking the summary for the stream name rhtpa-2.1.

assertion_results.text: Step 4.4 links the new CVE Jira TC-8021 to the preemptive task TC-8022 with 'Depend' link type (standard remediation linkage)
assertion_results.evidence: reconciliation.md lines 40-48 (section 4.4.4a) shows: "jira.create_link(inwardIssue: TC-8021, outwardIssue: TC-8022, type: "Depend")" and describes it as "the standard remediation linkage." The link state table on lines 89-93 confirms: "TC-8021 | TC-8022 | Depend | New standard remediation linkage (added by reconciliation)". Also confirmed in triage-outcome.md line 28.

assertion_results.text: Step 4.4 removes the 'security-preemptive' label from TC-8022 — the task is now a standard remediation task linked to a proper CVE Jira
assertion_results.evidence: reconciliation.md lines 55-63 (section 4.4.4b) shows removing security-preemptive label: updated_labels = ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123"] (without security-preemptive). Lines 97-101 (Label State After Reconciliation table) confirm: Before: "ai-generated-jira, Security, CVE-2026-55123, security-preemptive" -> After: "ai-generated-jira, Security, CVE-2026-55123". Line 102: "The `security-preemptive` label is removed, converting TC-8022 from a preemptive task to a standard remediation task."

assertion_results.text: Step 7 skips remediation task creation for this stream because Step 4.4 reconciliation already linked an existing task
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md lines 33-36: "Because Step 4.4 recorded that remediation for stream 2.1.x already exists (via reconciled TC-8022), Step 7 does not create new remediation tasks." Lines 37-38: "No new tasks created -- TC-8022 already covers the remediation (bump tokio to >= 1.42.0 on stream 2.1.x)". Also reconciliation.md lines 82-85 records: "Stream 2.1.x: remediation covered by TC-8022 (reconciled preemptive task) - No new remediation tasks needed for this stream".
